claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with a few questions:

**What is your name, and how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! My name is Sarah, and I'm inquiring about my three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, Sarah! It's helpful to know you're inquiring about three children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to gather some information about your circumstances.

**Let me start with the first question: How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Alex is 3 years old.

**How old is Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Blake is 8 years old.

**How old is Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie is 2 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So you have three young children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2).

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! You live in the UK.

**Do you live with all three of your children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! That's very helpful to know.

**Are any of your children currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital, care, or residential accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**Is there anyone else claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children, or are you the only person claiming for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm the only person claiming for them. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of my children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! That's very clear.

**Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, pre-settled status, or are you a British citizen/have the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Thank you for confirming that.

I believe I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie. Let me review your circumstances against the rules:

- You live in the UK ✓
- You live with all three children ✓
- You are responsible for all three children ✓
- Alex is 3 years old (under 16) ✓
- Blake is 8 years old (under 16) ✓
- Charlie is 2 years old (under 16) ✓
- None of the children are in hospital, care, or residential accommodation ✓
- You are a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK ✓
- You are the only person claiming for them ✓

Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you meet all the criteria for eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news, Sarah!** You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children:

✅ **Alex** - Eligible
✅ **Blake** - Eligible  
✅ **Charlie** - Eligible

You meet all the key eligibility requirements: you're a British citizen living in the UK, you're responsible for and live with all three children (who are all under 16), and you're the only person claiming for them.

If you haven't already done so, you can now proceed to claim Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with Sarah in the UK, and Sarah is responsible for Alex. Sarah is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with Sarah in the UK, and Sarah is responsible for Blake. Sarah is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. All eligibility criteria are met.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with Sarah in the UK, and Sarah is responsible for Charlie. Sarah is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Sarah is a British citizen living in the UK who is responsible for and lives with three children (Alex aged 3, Blake aged 8, and Charlie aged 2), all of whom are under 16. None of the children are in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Sarah is the sole person claiming for each child. She meets all the core eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for all three children."

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with Sarah in the UK, and Sarah is responsible for Blake. Sarah is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. All eligibility criteria are met.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with Sarah in the UK, and Sarah is responsible for Charlie. Sarah is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. All eligibility criteria are met.

None of the children are in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Sarah is the sole person claiming for each child. She meets all the core eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for all three children."
